statement: The liar’s punishment is that even when he speaks the truth, no one believes him
author: Sanhedrin 89b.

statement: Once a person has sinned and repeated the sin, [he treats it] as if it has become permitted
author: Rav Huna, Arachin 30b

statement: The inclination only desires that which is forbidden
author: Jerusalem Talmud, Nedarim 9:1.

statement: No two minds are alike, [just as] no two faces are alike
author: Berachot 58a.

statement: Do not celebrate among the mourners, and do not weep among revelers
author: Kallah 10.

statement: There is no community where everyone is rich; neither is there a community where everyone is poor
author: Jerusalem Talmud, Gitten 3:7.

statement: A person’s true character is ascertained by three parameters: his cup
author: i.e., his behavior when he drinks), his pocket

statement: Do not say something that should never be heard, because ultimately it will be heard
author: Hillel the Elder, Avot 4:2.

statement: Anyone who introduces excessive fear into his house will ultimately cause three sins: infidelity, murder, and Sabbath desecration
author: Gittin 6b.

statement: Be wary of authorities who befriend a person for their own purposes. They appear loving when it is beneficial to them, but do not stand by the other person in his time of distress
author: Rabban Gamliel, Avot 2:3.

statement: Youth is a crown of roses; old age is a crown of thorns
author: Rav Dimi, Shabbat 152a.

statement: When we were young [we saw ourselves] as men, now [we are considered] children
author: Bava Kama 92b.

statement: The world is a dark place for one who looks to the table of others [for his sustenance]
author: Rav, Beitza 32b.

statement: No one is [truly] poor, save for someone who is ill
author: Nedarim 4a.

statement: If a person says I have worked hard but have not found [success], don’t believe him. [If a person says] I have not exerted myself, and I have found [success], don’t believe him. [If he says] I gave it my all and I have found [success], believe him!
author: Megillah 6b.

statement: Do not appease a person while he is still angry
author: Avot 4:18.

statement: A prisoner cannot free himself
author: Berachot 5b.

statement: Just as you are obligated to speak when your words will be heeded, you must remain silent when you know your words will be ignored
author: Yevamot 65b.

statement: If you have guarded your mouth from evil speech, your days will be peaceful
author: Derech Eretz Zuta 9.

statement: Silence is beautiful for wise people; it is all the more beautiful for fools
author: Pesachim 99a.

statement: Even a poor person who subsists on the charity of others should perform [acts of] charity
author: Gittin 7a.
